Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on CentOS 7.9/x86_64 uploaded by AT Release Candidate from trevis-70vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/c9597024-835f-4a8f-bf79-a8f58e606a66 ran 4 tests.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Mark this -1 until at least the patch has landed to master.

Ideally, we would get the previous deprecation patch included into 2.12.7 and 2.14.0 and can then decide whether the backport of the total removal of "use_tcp_bonding" is worthwhile for the improved performance of conns_per_peer.

I suspect it will be worth it, but we need to give at least some time for the deprecation message to be visible.

Patch Set 1:

I'm fine with that. The reason I pushed this is so Cray can pick up the patches for their Lustre client.

parents.subject: LU-14838 tests: skip sanityn/32a if no truncate_lock

commit.subject: LU-13641 socklnd: remove tcp bonding
commit.message:
LU-13641 socklnd: remove tcp bonding

TCP bonding in the socklnd has become obsolete with LNet
Multi-Rail and there's no evidence it's being used anywhere.
Remove it to keep the code simple.

Lustre-change: https://review.whamcloud.com/40000
Lustre-commit: d123c47a18adbf5665ed63d99c53117b84db9ec8

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ib456f951b8ccd59112c460085632a2cb3c982004
